records.ety: [OE. dayesye, AS. dæges-ege day's eye, daisy. See Day, and Eye.]

defs: A genus of low herbs (Bellis), belonging to the family Compositæ. The common English and classical daisy is Bellis perennis, which has a yellow disk and white or pinkish rays.
The whiteweed (Chrysanthemum Leucanthemum), the plant commonly called daisy in North America; -- called also oxeye daisy. See Whiteweed.
notes: ☞ The word daisy is also used for composite plants of other genera, as Erigeron, or fleabane.
